data.title: How Do Mount Polley Results Reframe Imperial Metals' (TSX:III) Long-Term Copper Strategy?
data.description:

  • The company also released encouraging C2 zone and Bell Pit drill results that point to potential mine plan optimization and longer-term resource upside at Mount Polley.

What Is Imperial Metals' Investment Narrative?

To own Imperial Metals today, you need to believe that Mount Polley and Red Chris can keep translating decent production and high quality earnings into sustainable cash generation, while management steadily tidies up the balance sheet. The latest Mount Polley update slots into that story in a mixed way. On the positive side, beating 2025 copper targets and meeting gold guidance supports the recent improvement in margins and lends some credibility to management’s planning. The new C2 zone and Bell Pit drill results also refresh one of the key short term catalysts: proof that Mount Polley still has room for mine life and plan optimization. At the same time, the step down in 2026 copper guidance underlines the core risk here: output volatility at a time when the share price has already run very hard over the past year.

data.title: Ingersoll Rand (IR) Margin Compression Tests Bullish Earnings Growth Narratives
data.description:
Ingersoll Rand (IR) has wrapped up FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$2.1 billion and basic EPS of US$0.68, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$7.7 billion and EPS of US$1.46 that reflect the impact of a one off loss on profitability. The company has seen revenue move from US$1.90 billion and EPS of US$0.57 in Q4 2024 to US$2.1 billion and EPS of US$0.68 in Q4 2025, while trailing net margin has compressed from 11.6% to 7.6% as that US$273.4 million one time hit flows through the numbers. With analysts expecting earnings to grow materially faster than revenue over the next few years, these results set up a margins focused story where investors are watching how quickly profitability can rebuild from here.

See our full analysis for Ingersoll Rand.

With the headline results on the table, the next step is to see how these margins, growth expectations and one off items line up against the most widely held narratives about Ingersoll Rand and where those stories might need updating.

See what the community is saying about Ingersoll Rand

Advertisement

EPS swings underline mixed earnings picture

  • Across FY 2025, basic EPS moved from US$0.46 in Q1 to a loss of US$0.29 in Q2, then to US$0.62 in Q3 and US$0.68 in Q4, showing that the one off US$273.4 million loss sat alongside a wide range of quarterly profitability outcomes.
  • What bullish investors are leaning on is the view that, despite this bumpy EPS path and trailing basic EPS of US$1.46, earnings can grow about 24.2% per year while revenue is forecast at about 5.7% per year. This assumes margin rebuilding on top of a revenue base that has gone from US$1,898.6 million in Q4 2024 to US$2,091.2 million in Q4 2025.
    • Bulls point to expansion into underpenetrated regions and an active M&A program that aims to add 400 to 500 basis points of annualized inorganic revenue in 2025 as support for those faster earnings forecasts.
    • The same bullish view expects profit margins to rise from recent levels toward the mid teens over the next few years, which would be a clear shift from the current trailing net margin of 7.6% that still reflects the one off loss.

Bulls argue that the recent EPS volatility is a temporary step on the way to higher profit margins and faster earnings growth, so if you want to see how that case is built out in full, you can read the detailed bull story here 🐂 Ingersoll Rand Bull Case

Margins under pressure while growth is forecast to accelerate

  • Over the last 12 months, trailing revenue is US$7.7b and trailing net income excluding extra items is US$581.4 million, which works out to a 7.6% net margin compared with 11.6% a year earlier. The current margin base is therefore lower even as analysts expect earnings to grow about 24.2% per year.
  • Bears focus on this margin compression, arguing that the push into new regions and reliance on acquisitions, combined with supply chain and China related pressures, could keep profitability under strain even if revenue continues to trend from US$1,861 million in Q3 2024 to levels around US$2,091.2 million most recently.
    • Critics highlight that weaker volumes and declines in China have already affected certain segments, which lines up with the trailing margin slipping to 7.6% despite revenue growth.
    • Bears also flag that integration costs and potential new tariffs or trade frictions could add to expenses, making it harder for margins to move back toward the low double digit levels referenced in forward looking narratives.

Skeptics warn that the current 7.6% margin and recent regional headwinds leave less room for error, so if you want to see how the cautious case lines this up with future earnings assumptions, it is worth reading the bear side in detail 🐻 Ingersoll Rand Bear Case

Rich valuation leans on forecasts, not trailing numbers

  • With the share price at US$98.52, the stock trades on a P/E of 66.3x based on trailing earnings, compared with peer and industry averages around 30x, and a DCF fair value of about US$89.61 that sits below the current price.
  • The balanced narrative around the stock points out that, for this higher P/E to make sense, the forecast gap between roughly 24.2% annual earnings growth and about 5.7% annual revenue growth needs to be backed by a sustained lift in margins from the current 7.6% level, rather than just one off adjustments.
    • Supporters of the consensus view note that recurring, higher margin aftermarket revenue, which has reached 37% of total, is intended to help drive that margin improvement over time.
    • At the same time, the presence of a large US$273.4 million one off loss and prior goodwill and asset impairments shows how acquisition heavy growth can feed through to reported profitability and feeds into the caution embedded in the DCF fair value of US$89.61.

Next Steps

data.title: Robinhood Chain Puts Tokenized Assets And Prediction Markets At Center Stage
data.description:
• Robinhood Markets launched Robinhood Chain, its own Ethereum Layer-2 blockchain, as it pushes further into on-chain trading.
  • The company is pairing this with a bigger move into prediction markets and tokenized assets, including a stake in a derivatives exchange and a planned joint venture with Susquehanna.
  • The goal is to bring real-world assets like stocks and ETFs on chain with 24/7 trading, instant settlement, and self-custody.

Robinhood Markets (NasdaqGS:HOOD) is making this move at a time when its share price is $75.97, with a 16.4% return over the past year. The stock has seen a 7 day return of an 8.3% decline and a 30 day return of a 36.5% decline, and is also down 34.1% year to date, so the market has recently been repricing expectations around the business.

The launch of Robinhood Chain and the push into prediction markets and tokenized assets show the company leaning further into the crossover between its brokerage roots and on chain finance. For you as an investor, the key questions are how quickly these products gain traction, how they affect revenue mix beyond crypto trading, and what that could mean for NasdaqGS:HOOD over time compared with more traditional brokerages.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Robinhood Markets by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Robinhood Markets.

2 things going right for Robinhood Markets that this headline doesn't cover.

For Robinhood, launching Robinhood Chain right after a mixed fourth quarter puts more weight on whether new products can diversify revenue away from crypto trading. Q4 revenue of US$1.28b and net income of US$605m came in with softer growth than many investors were hoping for, and crypto transaction revenue declined 38% year on year, which helps explain the recent share price reset. At the same time, full year 2025 revenue of US$4.47b and net income of US$1.88b show that the broader business is still generating sizeable profit, supported by options, prediction markets and subscriptions.

Robinhood Chain sits at the intersection of those trends. If tokenized stocks, ETFs and prediction markets settle on Robinhood’s own Ethereum Layer 2 rather than third party rails, Robinhood gains more control over fees, margins and product design. That could matter for competitive positioning against players like Coinbase, Interactive Brokers and Charles Schwab, especially as the company pushes for 24/7 trading and instant settlement. The buyback program, with US$909.51m spent to repurchase about 2.5% of shares, also shows management is willing to return capital while funding this push into tokenization and on chain trading.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Robinhood Markets Narrative

  • Robinhood Chain directly supports the narrative that tokenized assets and prediction markets can become new revenue pillars that broaden Robinhood’s product set and help it evolve beyond a single product trading app.
  • The reliance on regulatory progress for tokenization and event contracts also highlights a narrative risk, as stricter rules or slower approvals could limit how quickly Robinhood can scale these offerings.
  • The shift to owning more of the on chain infrastructure via Robinhood Chain is only lightly reflected in the existing narrative and may not fully capture potential changes in unit economics versus relying on external crypto venues.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Robinhood Markets to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Heavy exposure to crypto and on chain activity, at a time when crypto transaction revenue fell 38% in Q4, means results can be sensitive to digital asset trading volumes.
  • ⚠️ Building prediction markets and tokenized assets into core products depends on regulatory approvals, where changes to rules for event contracts or tokenized securities could slow adoption or add compliance costs.
  • 🎁 Robinhood’s record full year revenue of US$4.47b and net income of US$1.88b provide a profit base that can help fund Robinhood Chain, prediction markets and international expansion without relying solely on external capital.
  • 🎁 The company is widening its product set across options, prediction markets, banking and tokenized assets, which may reduce reliance on any single revenue line and deepen customer relationships over time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to watch how quickly users and developers adopt Robinhood Chain, especially once stock and ETF tokens migrate over, and whether prediction market volumes continue to grow on top of that infrastructure. Tracking the mix of transaction revenue between crypto, options, prediction markets and tokenized assets will show whether this launch actually diversifies the business. It is also worth keeping an eye on any updates from regulators on tokenization and event contracts, plus how competitors like Coinbase and traditional brokers respond to 24/7, on chain trading.

data.title: Klabin S.A. Just Missed EPS By 15%: Here's What Analysts Think Will Happen Next
data.description:
It's been a good week for Klabin S.A. (BVMF:KLBN11) shareholders, because the company has just released its latest annual results, and the shares gained 5.6% to R$20.39. It was not a great result overall. While revenues of R$21b were in line with analyst predictions, earnings were less than expected, missing statutory estimates by 15% to hit R$1.14 per share. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

After the latest results, the 14 analysts covering Klabin are now predicting revenues of R$21.7b in 2026. If met, this would reflect a satisfactory 5.0% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to jump 56% to R$1.78. In the lead-up to this report, the analysts had been modelling revenues of R$21.6b and earnings per share (EPS) of R$2.01 in 2026. The analysts seem to have become more bearish following the latest results. While there were no changes to revenue forecasts, there was a substantial drop in EPS estimates.

View our latest analysis for Klabin

The consensus price target held steady at R$23.89, with the analysts seemingly voting that their lower forecast earnings are not expected to lead to a lower stock price in the foreseeable future. The consensus price target is just an average of individual analyst targets, so - it could be handy to see how wide the range of underlying estimates is. Currently, the most bullish analyst values Klabin at R$29.00 per share, while the most bearish prices it at R$18.00. There are definitely some different views on the stock, but the range of estimates is not wide enough as to imply that the situation is unforecastable, in our view.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that Klabin's revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2026 expected to display 5.0% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 7.5% over the past five years. Juxtapose this against the other companies in the industry with analyst coverage, which are forecast to grow their revenues (in aggregate) 4.1% annually. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it looks like Klabin is forecast to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analysts downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. They also reconfirmed their revenue estimates, with the company predicted to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry. There was no real change to the consensus price target, suggesting that the intrinsic value of the business has not undergone any major changes with the latest estimates.

Keeping that in mind, we still think that the longer term trajectory of the business is much more important for investors to consider. We have estimates - from multiple Klabin analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

data.title: Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário's (BVMF:PLPL3) Returns Have Hit A Wall
data.description:
If we want to find a potential multi-bagger, often there are underlying trends that can provide clues. Firstly, we'd want to identify a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and then alongside that, an ever-increasing base of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. That's why when we briefly looked at Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário's (BVMF:PLPL3) ROCE trend, we were pretty happy with what we saw.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Understanding Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)

For those who don't know, ROCE is a measure of a company's yearly pre-tax profit (its return), relative to the capital employed in the business. The formula for this calculation on Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.16 = R$466m ÷ (R$3.8b - R$894m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Therefore, Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has an ROCE of 16%. On its own, that's a standard return, however it's much better than the 8.7% generated by the Consumer Durables industry.

Check out our latest analysis for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free analyst report for Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário .

What The Trend Of ROCE Can Tell Us

While the current returns on capital are decent, they haven't changed much. The company has consistently earned 16% for the last five years, and the capital employed within the business has risen 262% in that time. 16% is a pretty standard return, and it provides some comfort knowing that Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has consistently earned this amount. Over long periods of time, returns like these might not be too exciting, but with consistency they can pay off in terms of share price returns.

In Conclusion...

The main thing to remember is that Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário has proven its ability to continually reinvest at respectable rates of return. On top of that, the stock has rewarded shareholders with a remarkable 241% return to those who've held over the last five years. So while investors seem to be recognizing these promising trends, we still believe the stock deserves further research.

One more thing to note, we've identified 1 warning sign with Plano & Plano Desenvolvimento Imobiliário and understanding it should be part of your investment process.

data.title: Investors Met With Slowing Returns on Capital At Bastide Le Confort Médical (EPA:BLC)
data.description:
Did you know there are some financial metrics that can provide clues of a potential multi-bagger? Amongst other things, we'll want to see two things; firstly, a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an expansion in the company's amount of capital employed. If you see this, it typically means it's a company with a great business model and plenty of profitable reinvestment opportunities. However, after briefly looking over the numbers, we don't think Bastide Le Confort Médical (EPA:BLC) has the makings of a multi-bagger going forward, but let's have a look at why that may be.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. The formula for this calculation on Bastide Le Confort Médical is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.098 = €45m ÷ (€661m - €200m) (Based on the trailing twelve months to June 2025).

Thus, Bastide Le Confort Médical has an ROCE of 9.8%. On its own, that's a low figure but it's around the 8.7% average generated by the Healthcare industry.

See our latest analysis for Bastide Le Confort Médical

Above you can see how the current ROCE for Bastide Le Confort Médical compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering Bastide Le Confort Médical for free.

The Trend Of ROCE

In terms of Bastide Le Confort Médical's historical ROCE trend, it doesn't exactly demand attention. The company has employed 27% more capital in the last five years, and the returns on that capital have remained stable at 9.8%. Given the company has increased the amount of capital employed, it appears the investments that have been made simply don't provide a high return on capital.

What We Can Learn From Bastide Le Confort Médical's ROCE

In summary, Bastide Le Confort Médical has simply been reinvesting capital and generating the same low rate of return as before. And in the last five years, the stock has given away 55% so the market doesn't look too hopeful on these trends strengthening any time soon. In any case, the stock doesn't have these traits of a multi-bagger discussed above, so if that's what you're looking for, we think you'd have more luck elsewhere.

data.title: Boule Diagnostics AB (publ) Just Missed Earnings With A Surprise Loss - Here Are Analysts Latest Forecasts
data.description:
There's been a notable change in appetite for Boule Diagnostics AB (publ) (STO:BOUL) shares in the week since its annual report, with the stock down 12% to kr4.41. It was a pretty negative result overall, with revenues of kr490m missing analyst predictions by 2.7%. Worse, the business reported a statutory loss of kr0.10 per share, a substantial decline on analyst expectations of a profit. The analyst typically update their forecasts at each earnings report, and we can judge from their estimates whether their view of the company has changed or if there are any new concerns to be aware of. With this in mind, we've gathered the latest statutory forecasts to see what the analyst is expecting for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Following last week's earnings report, Boule Diagnostics' single analyst are forecasting 2026 revenues to be kr498.8m, approximately in line with the last 12 months. Earnings are expected to improve, with Boule Diagnostics forecast to report a statutory profit of kr0.82 per share. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analyst had been anticipated revenues of kr535.2m and earnings per share (EPS) of kr0.92 in 2026. From this we can that sentiment has definitely become more bearish after the latest results, leading to lower revenue forecasts and a substantial drop in earnings per share estimates.

Check out our latest analysis for Boule Diagnostics

The consensus price target fell 13% to kr6.50, with the weaker earnings outlook clearly leading valuation estimates.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. It's pretty clear that there is an expectation that Boule Diagnostics' revenue growth will slow down substantially, with revenues to the end of 2026 expected to display 1.9% growth on an annualised basis. This is compared to a historical growth rate of 5.1% over the past five years. Compare this against other companies (with analyst forecasts) in the industry, which are in aggregate expected to see revenue growth of 13% annually. So it's pretty clear that, while revenue growth is expected to slow down, the wider industry is also expected to grow faster than Boule Diagnostics.

The Bottom Line

The most important thing to take away is that the analyst downgraded their earnings per share estimates, showing that there has been a clear decline in sentiment following these results. On the negative side, they also downgraded their revenue estimates, and forecasts imply they will perform worse than the wider industry. The consensus price target fell measurably, with the analyst seemingly not reassured by the latest results, leading to a lower estimate of Boule Diagnostics' future valuation.

data.title: Picton Property Income Limited's (LON:PCTN) Has Been On A Rise But Financial Prospects Look Weak: Is The Stock Overpriced?
data.description:
Picton Property Income (LON:PCTN) has had a great run on the share market with its stock up by a significant 18% over the last three months. We, however wanted to have a closer look at its key financial indicators as the markets usually pay for long-term fundamentals, and in this case, they don't look very promising. In this article, we decided to focus on Picton Property Income's ROE.

ROE or return on equity is a useful tool to assess how effectively a company can generate returns on the investment it received from its shareholders. Simply put, it is used to assess the profitability of a company in relation to its equity capital.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

How Do You Calculate Return On Equity?

The formula for ROE is:

Return on Equity = Net Profit (from continuing operations) ÷ Shareholders' Equity

So, based on the above formula, the ROE for Picton Property Income is:

7.8% = UK£41m ÷ UK£528m (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

The 'return' is the profit over the last twelve months. One way to conceptualize this is that for each £1 of shareholders' capital it has, the company made £0.08 in profit.

Check out our latest analysis for Picton Property Income

What Has ROE Got To Do With Earnings Growth?

We have already established that ROE serves as an efficient profit-generating gauge for a company's future earnings. We now need to evaluate how much profit the company reinvests or "retains" for future growth which then gives us an idea about the growth potential of the company. Assuming everything else remains unchanged, the higher the ROE and profit retention, the higher the growth rate of a company compared to companies that don't necessarily bear these characteristics.

A Side By Side comparison of Picton Property Income's Earnings Growth And 7.8% ROE

At first glance, Picton Property Income's ROE doesn't look very promising. Yet, a closer study shows that the company's ROE is similar to the industry average of 7.7%. But Picton Property Income saw a five year net income decline of 22% over the past five years. Remember, the company's ROE is a bit low to begin with. So that's what might be causing earnings growth to shrink.

However, when we compared Picton Property Income's growth with the industry we found that while the company's earnings have been shrinking, the industry has seen an earnings growth of 1.4% in the same period. This is quite worrisome.

The basis for attaching value to a company is, to a great extent, tied to its earnings growth. What investors need to determine next is if the expected earnings growth, or the lack of it, is already built into the share price. This then helps them determine if the stock is placed for a bright or bleak future. Is Picton Property Income fairly valued compared to other companies? These 3 valuation measures might help you decide.

Is Picton Property Income Using Its Retained Earnings Effectively?

Picton Property Income has a very high three-year median payout ratio of 86%, implying that it retains only 14% of its profits. However, it's not unusual to see a REIT with such a high payout ratio mainly due to statutory requirements. Accordingly, this likely explains why its earnings have been shrinking.

Moreover, Picton Property Income has been paying dividends for at least ten years or more suggesting that management must have perceived that the shareholders prefer dividends over earnings growth.

Summary

On the whole, Picton Property Income's performance is quite a big let-down. The company has seen a lack of earnings growth as a result of retaining very little profits and whatever little it does retain, is being reinvested at a very low rate of return. Having said that, we studied the latest analyst forecasts, and found that analysts are expecting the company's earnings growth to improve slightly. This could offer some relief to the company's existing shareholders. Are these analysts expectations based on the broad expectations for the industry, or on the company's fundamentals?
